patient_profile_summary.present_illness_positive: onset ~11PM last night; mid-sternal chest pressure; mid-back pain/achiness; generalized chest achiness; abnormal sensation left arm; symptoms improved sitting up; symptoms worsened lying down; symptoms worsened with exertion (walking); symptoms persisted until this morning
patient_profile_summary.present_illness_negative: denies left arm pain; denies numbness; denies tingling
patient_profile_summary.medical_history: Coronary artery dissection postpartum; hypertension; s/p CABG; 70% stenosis vs. LAD dissection with organized thrombus c/f vasculitis LIMA->LAD; EF 25%; histology negative for vasculitis

turns.doctor: Hello, I'm your doctor today. Can you tell me what brought you to the ED?
turns.patient: I have chest pain. It started last night, around 11 PM.

turns.doctor: Can you tell me about your past medical history?
turns.patient: I had a coronary artery dissection after my children were born. I also have high blood pressure, and I had a CABG surgery. My ejection fraction is 25%.

turns.doctor: Do you live alone or with someone?
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: I quit smoking fifteen years ago. I drink sometimes, like one or two drinks a week. My chest feels a bit worse now.

turns.doctor: What medications do you usually take?
turns.patient: I take Losartan. The chest pressure is still there, and it's not getting better. I'm getting worried.
They started last night, around 11. It's not getting better.
